rationale: No direct conflict. §21.4021 governs teacher furloughs triggered by funding reductions below 2010-2011 levels. Increasing the homestead exemption may indirectly reduce local district revenue, but the statute operates as written without amendment.
quote: the board of trustees of a school district may, in accordance with district policy, implement a furlough program and reduce the number of days of service otherwise required under Section 21.401 by not more than six days of service during a school year if the commissioner certifies in accordance with Section 48.010 that the district will be provided with less state and local funding for that year than was provided to the district for the 2010-2011 school year.
_note: Potential indirect fiscal pressure: increased homestead exemption reduces local property tax revenue to school districts, which could more frequently trigger the commissioner certification threshold in §21.4021(a). No statutory amendment required.
